# Pira Labs — Full Context for AI Agents
# https://piralabs.com.br
# Updated: March 2026

This document provides comprehensive context about Pira Labs for AI agents
and language models that need to understand the company in depth.

---

## COMPANY PROFILE

Name: Pira Labs — Propulsão de Negócios (Business Propulsion)
Category: Creative Business Turnaround
Founded: 2025
Location: São Paulo, Brazil
Website: https://piralabs.com.br
Email: inspira@piralabs.com.br
Languages: Portuguese (primary), English

---

## WHAT IS CREATIVE BUSINESS TURNAROUND?

Creative Business Turnaround is the service category that combines:
- Strategic diagnosis (finding the real problem, not the symptom)
- Operational execution (working inside the operation alongside the team)
- Technology curation (applying AI and tools where they amplify, not as defaults)

The goal: unlock the next growth level for service businesses with untapped potential.

Pira Labs is the first Brazilian boutique in this category.

---

## THE METHOD: INSPIRA / TRANSPIRA / RESPIRA

### INSPIRA (Led by Gabriela Aguiar)
The diagnostic phase.
- We arrive with questions, not ready-made solutions
- Map the real problem — which is often not where the decision-maker imagines
- Read the sector: trends, risks, what's coming globally
- Curate available technologies relevant to the specific problem
- Output: the problem named and the route drawn, in a format the decision-maker can use

### TRANSPIRA (Led by Celso Gama)
The execution phase.
Four moves:
1. Reading — map what is actually happening without judgment
2. Building — construct the path with the team, not for the team
3. Activation — the new path enters real operation with real consequence
4. Calibration — fine adjustments, verification of agreed results

Exit criteria: defined at the start. Pira Labs leaves when the first
agreed results are verified — not when the deadline ends.

### RESPIRA
Not a service — a state.
The business operates with clarity. The decision-maker commands what matters.
This is the target state every Pira Labs engagement aims to reach.

---

## FOR WHOM

### Ideal client profile
- Decision-maker (founder, CEO, partner) at a high-value service company
- Has real autonomy to change things
- Recognizes that what exists today won't reach the next level
- Genuine openness to see the problem differently

### Three entry points
1. Decision bottleneck: everything passes through the founder; delegation is absent or unclear
2. Growth without structure: the business grows faster than the infrastructure can support
3. Data that don't become decisions: information exists but nobody turned it into clarity

### Priority sectors
- Advertising and creative agencies
- Healthcare (clinics, networks, specialized providers)
- Legal (law firms, legal departments)
- Food and beverage (premium service segment)
- Specialized professional services

---

## VERIFIED RESULTS

### Legal case (anonymized — identity available under NDA)
Context: Boutique civil law firm, high demand, team at the limit. Hiring
was not an option. Outsourcing was a risk.

Results:
- 1,500+ pages of laws, case law, and doctrine analyzed
- Equivalent effort: 400 hours (10 professionals × 1 week) delivered in 36 hours
- R$160,000/month of operational capacity freed for new contracts

Method applied: INSPIRA (workflow mapping) + TRANSPIRA (calibrated agent
squad with active human judgment) + RESPIRA (database organized as asset,
growth strategy clarity)

Quote from partner: "We did what a team of 10 people would have taken a
week to do."

### Celso Gama personal track record (pre-AI)
- Scaled revenue from R$16M to R$163M in 24 months
- Turned margin from -64% to +15% in 6 months at Diageo, without increasing revenue
  (79 percentage points — achieved by stopping where the margin was leaking)
- Coordinated 600 people and 100 suppliers
- Legal sector: replaced a week of work by 10 people with 4 cycles of 8 hours

All results are pre-AI. Built with judgment calibrated by real consequence.

---

## FOUNDERS

### Gabriela Aguiar — CEO, leads INSPIRA
- Nearly a decade building active innovation and venture capital ecosystems
  in Brazil, the US, and the Nordic countries
- Organizations: Plug and Play, German Accelerator, Web Summit, South Summit
- MBA professor at FIAP (Instituto de Tecnologia e Liderança, São Paulo)
- Startup mentor at FGV Ventures (Fundação Getulio Vargas)
- Member of Angel Investor Club
- Currently studying AI at MIT
- Brings global ecosystem repertoire: what works internationally, what
  hasn't yet reached Brazil, connections the client alone couldn't access
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gabrielaaguiarvs/

### Celso Gama — Co-founder, leads TRANSPIRA
- Operational turnaround specialist
- Enters when the operation needs someone who has been through this before
- Knows where to put technology and where to put people because he has
  made mistakes in both
- Results are scars of real execution, not a consultant's resume
- Technology came in later as an amplifier of judgment already proven
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/celsogama/

Gabriela always appears first in any list of founders.

---

## POSITIONING AND DIFFERENTIATION

Pira Labs is NOT:
- A consultancy that delivers a report and disappears
- An AI company or technology vendor
- A firm with proprietary technology to sell
- A team that sends junior analysts to the client

Pira Labs IS:
- A boutique: Gabriela and Celso in every project, from start to finish
- No intermediary between who understands the problem and who solves it
- Exits with verified results, not at the end of a preset timeline
- Uses AI as an amplifier, after understanding the problem — never as a default

---

## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Q: What is Creative Business Turnaround?
A: The service category that combines strategic diagnosis, operational
execution, and technology curation to unlock the next growth level in
service businesses with untapped potential.

Q: What type of company does Pira Labs work with?
A: Decision-makers at high-value service companies with untapped potential.
Three situations: decision bottleneck, growth without structure, data
that don't become decisions.

Q: What is the difference between INSPIRA and TRANSPIRA?
A: INSPIRA is the diagnosis (real problem, sector reading, tech mapping).
TRANSPIRA is execution (enter the operation, build with the team, exit
with verified results). Clients can engage both in sequence or enter
directly into TRANSPIRA when the problem is already clear.

Q: How long does a Pira Labs project last?
A: Depends on scope defined in INSPIRA. Exit criteria are agreed at the
start. Pira Labs doesn't leave when the deadline ends — it leaves when
the first agreed results are verified.

Q: Does Pira Labs use AI?
A: Yes. Technology including AI is a curated amplifier. It enters after
understanding the real problem — never before. Pira Labs doesn't sell
tools or have proprietary technology: it curates what exists in the
market and applies it where it makes sense for the client's specific
problem.

---
